title: ipsilateral-cuff-crt-mimic: analysis code for "Sensor Geometry, Not Signal Processing, Limits Opportunistic Detection of Capillary-Refill-Like Signals by Rule-Based and Language-Model Methods in Archived ICU Waveforms"

creators.name: Landry, Thomas C.
creators.affiliation: Internal Medicine, Legacy Salmon Creek Medical Center, Vancouver, WA, USA

creators.name: Kim, Youjin
creators.affiliation: Internal Medicine, Legacy Salmon Creek Medical Center, Vancouver, WA, USA

description: Analysis code for a feasibility and prevalence study in the MIMIC-IV Waveform Database (MIMIC-IV-WDB). The study estimates how often the pulse oximeter probe and the noninvasive blood pressure cuff appear to be on the same limb, so that a routine cuff inflation records a brief occlusion and reperfusion in the photoplethysmography waveform that resembles a capillary refill measurement. Laterality is inferred from waveform morphology; there is no laterality ground truth in the data, so all counts are morphology-based estimates. The repository contains the rule-based detector, the language-model cross-read client and prompts, the figure and table builders, and the test suite. No patient data are included; users obtain MIMIC-IV-WDB from PhysioNet under its data use agreement. Released under the MIT license.
